body:
Lower priority than AR-0001 and AR-0002, but worth checking while you have context.

Our SSSSL window pattern is [256,256,256,256,2048,256,256,256,2048]. Read engine/src/forward.rs and look at how WINDOW_SIZES[layer] is applied to the Flash Attention call.

Questions:
1. Does the sliding window mask interact correctly with causal masking? (i.e., token at position i can attend to max(0, i-window)..i)
2. For window=256, are we passing window_size=256 or window_size=255 to FA3? Off-by-one here could subtly change what the model learns.
3. Does Python use the same window semantics? Check original/train.py for how window_size is passed to the attention function.

This is a potential hidden bug that would NOT show up in single-batch forward pass comparison (since both would be consistent within themselves) but could explain a training quality gap.
